title: Questions that Matter: Lessons on the good life from a Star Trek opera-in-progress
summary: For the past decade, an eclectic group of professors, staff members, and show-business professionals has been working hard to bring a Star Trek opera to vibrant life. Now, they are inviting the public to delve more deeply into the philosophical heart of their interstellar project. Their talk, “How to Live Long and Prosper: Lessons from a Star Trek Opera,” challenges the audience to consider a deceptively simple question: What do we need to lead a fulfilling life?

title: Mellon Foundation renews and doubles support for the Employing Humanities initiative
summary: Since its launch in 2023, the Employing Humanities initiative has been a resounding success, supporting hundreds of undergraduates and providing more than $1 million to bolster the initiative, which provides paid opportunities for students.Now, it is poised to have an even greater impact. The Mellon Foundation has approved a $2 million grant renewal to the program, doubling its investment and enabling Employing Humanities to expand its reach.

title: The creation of the Cigarette Surfboard
summary: Alumnus Ben Judkins’s (Kresge ’17, film and digital media) award-winning documentary follows the creation of the Cigarette Surfboard: A surfboard created by friend Taylor Lane from 10,000+ cigarette butts collected off beaches. Judkins’s documentary has helped influence legislation in Santa Cruz County, and will be screened at UC Santa Cruz on March 8.

title: The Lionel Cantú Queer Center food pantry seeks community support
summary: The Lionel Cantú Queer Center seeks donations as student demand hits record levels. Help sustain their 24/7 food pantry by donating nonperishable items to support student wellness.

title: Brain organoids can be trained to solve a goal-directed task
summary: UC Santa Cruz researchers are exploring how brains learn, adapt, and improve, which could help us better understand and address neurological conditions.

title: Observing and celebrating Ramadan, Passover, and Easter
summary: Students observing these holy days may miss class or request adjustments to assignments or scheduled assessments. Some may limit or avoid the use of technology during certain portions of their observance.

title: How corporations and politicians decide what you eat: a conversation with Marion Nestle
summary: A few hours before delivering the Peggy Downes Baskin Ethics Lecture, the longtime nutrition scholar and tireless consumer advocate Marion Nestle gave a far-ranging, informal interview at The Humanities Institute (THI.) Nestle discussed the political structure of the American food system, arguing that corporate influence—from agricultural subsidies to supermarket slotting fees—shapes what people eat far more than personal choice does.

title: Two promising technology development projects receive UC Santa Cruz Innovation Catalyst grants
summary: UC Santa Cruz’s Innovation Catalyst Proof-of-Concept Program helps move cutting-edge research from the lab out into the world for societal impact.

title: Secure AI tools now available to staff
summary: We recognize that the rapid growth of generative AI raises important considerations, including bias, ethical use, and environmental impact. AI tools can be powerful, but they require care – particularly around what information you share and how you use the results.

title: Information about Ramadan at UCSC
summary: Information about dining options, prayer spaces, accommodations, and programming for students observing Ramadan.

title: For 35 years, UCSC Adventure Rec has brought the Banff Mountain Film Festival to Santa Cruz — and opened the outdoors to students.
summary: For 35 years, UCSC Adventure Recreation has hosted the Banff Mountain Film Festival in Santa Cruz, turning world-class adventure films into direct support for students. Ticket sales help reduce trip costs by up to 50%, expanding access to outdoor leadership and recreation. Join the community February 27, 28 and March 1, 2026 at the Rio Theatre.

title: MIKE to headline exclusive hip hop concert for UCSC students
summary: New York–based rapper and producer MIKE will headline a student-only hip hop concert at the UCSC Quarry Amphitheater on Saturday, March 7, as part of After Hours, a new event series designed to create more opportunities for student connection, creativity, and engagement. The concert marks the first live music event in the After Hours series and is envisioned as the start of an annual concert tradition for UC Santa Cruz students.

title: Science Division awards honor staff and faculty for outstanding work and dedication
summary: Alyssa Danielli, department manager in astronomy and astrophysics, and Carrie Partch, professor of chemistry and biochemistry, are the respective recipients of the division’s 2024-25 Outstanding Staff and Faculty Awards

title: Baskin Engineering students win in sustainability, justice, and education projects at CruzHacks 2026
summary: CruzHacks 2026, UC Santa Cruz’s premier annual hackathon, brought together more than 300 students across 90 teams to engineer solutions with positive social impact. Notably, Baskin School of Engineering students contributed to three of the four main track–winning teams.

title: Annual notice on employees responsible for reporting discrimination, sexual violence, or sexual harassment
summary: UC Santa Cruz strives to create and sustain an equitable environment for students, staff, and faculty.

title: Why more UC Santa Cruz students are graduating in three years
summary: Three-year graduation is becoming more common at UC Santa Cruz, where intentional planning and advising have helped raise the three-year graduation rate to 10.3 percent, the highest in the University of California system.

title: Biochemists establish new method for identifying pharmaceutical candidates faster
summary: UC Santa Cruz team achieves synthesis of beneficial neurochemical in just two steps, promising to break a bottleneck in drug development

title: UCAD Plus Joint Task Force update #2
summary: UCOP continues to work diligently toward the goal of sharing preliminary recommendations with UC President Milliken, the Chancellors, and Senate faculty throughout the system by the end of this academic year.

title: Upcoming campus visits for CPEVC finalists
summary: I invite everyone in our campus community to participate in the process and provide feedback on the finalists.

title: Greener Greenhouses — a Q&A with Katia Obraczka
summary: UC Santa Cruz professor Katia Obraczka’s Greener Greenhouses project uses energy-efficient IoT technology to precisely monitor greenhouse conditions, helping growers reduce water and energy use while improving crop production.

title: Immersion in childhood cancer research transforms undergrads into scientists—and advocates
summary: The Treehouse Childhood Cancer Initiative is giving 80 students the opportunity to become cancer researchers for a quarter, thanks in part to generous funding from the UCSC Degree-Defining Experiences Program and the Dragon Master Initiative.

title: Notice of Open Comment: UC Santa Cruz Key Control Policy
summary: The Division of Finance, Operations, and Administration (FOA) is accepting comments on proposed updates to the Key Control policy.

title: Proposed increases to parking permits and program rates
summary: As part of a multiyear strategy to expand parking capacity while advancing campus sustainability and budgetary goals, the University of California, Santa Cruz is proposing targeted adjustments to TAPS parking permit and program rates and offerings, effective July 1, 2026.

title: How early pregnancy impacts aging: implications for breast-cancer risk
summary: New study by UC Santa Cruz team discovers that early pregnancy in mice reduces buildup of ‘confused’ cells that could lead to breast cancer later in life

title: SAB Winter Forum on February 26, 2026
summary: The Staff Advisory Board invites you to attend their Winter Forum with Chief Financial Officer & Vice Chancellor for Finance, Operations, and Administration Ed Reiskin and Associate Vice Chancellor for Budget Analysis and Planning Amber Blakeslee.

title: Distinguished Professor of Literature Carla Freccero dies at 69
summary: Carla Freccero, distinguished professor and chair of the Literature Department and a beloved mentor to hundreds of graduate students, died this week at Stanford Hospital, surrounded by loved ones. She was 69.

title: Distinguished Professor Emerita Judit Moschkovich elected to the National Academy of Education
summary: Moschkovich has published influential work advancing understanding of mathematical thinking, learning, and classroom discourse, particularly in linguistically diverse settings.

title: Meet this historian of alien worlds
summary: With equations and simulations, astronomer Ruth Murray-Clay is working to understand how distant planets form, evolve and could offer conditions to support life.

title: Regents endorse Inspiring Change fundraising campaign
summary: The University of California Board of Regents lent its support to Inspiring Change: The Campaign for UC Santa Cruz during its Jan. 21 meeting at UCLA, formally voting to endorse the fundraising campaign.  Inspiring Change: The Campaign for UC Santa Cruz is a comprehensive effort to raise $750 million by the end of 2030 so […]

title: Ensuring equitable technological transitions: AI use in the workforce
summary: While much of the public conversation about generative AI and work focuses on job replacement or increased productivity, Chris Benner’s research is taking clues from prior technological transitions to understand what other improvements are possible.

title: Vice Provost and Dean of Undergraduate Education search launches
summary: The Vice Provost and Dean of Undergraduate Education (VPDUE) provides campuswide leadership in sustaining and enhancing the quality of undergraduate academic experience.

title: A vision of freedom: the overlooked Black women activists who rallied for the vote in the Pacific Northwest
summary: Long before women gained the right to vote nationwide in 1920, Black women in the Pacific Northwest were already working to shape political life—organizing clubs, building party networks, and mobilizing voters.Assistant Professor of History Quin’Nita Cobbins-Modica’s research shines a light on these overlooked political strategists. Her scholarship has earned her the 2025 Judith Lee Ridge Award, which recognizes the best article in any field of history published by a member of the Western Association of Women Historians (WAWH).

title: Community Alert: Lewd act reported on campus
summary: The UC Santa Cruz Police Department is asking the community to call 911 if they see on campus an individual suspected of committing a lewd act.

title: Notice of open comment for interim Major Events Policy
summary: The Major Events Policy aims to prioritize public safety, coordinate resources, and provide better tools for event planners.

title: From founding faculty to future generations
summary: Celebrating Jim Pepper’s 50-year legacy of teaching, mentorship, and environmental leadership

title: Art confronts climate: A creative force in the environmental conversation
summary: Groundbreaking art and science collaborations are reimagining how we understand—and survive in—a warming world.

title: Misleading text in the physical world can hijack AI-enabled robots, cybersecurity study shows
summary: New research anticipates hijacking against AI systems in order to create defenses for a more secure future.

title: Prevent the spread of respiratory and stomach viruses
summary: As winter illnesses rise, prioritize your health and well-being with simple steps that protect you, your classmates, and campus community.

title: American Film Institute tapped UC Santa Cruz scholar to determine the top television programs for 2025
summary: Associate Professor of Film and Digital Media L.S. Kim joined critics, actors, producers, and writers on the American Film Institute’s Television Jury to formulate a list of the year’s outstanding television programs

title: Five student projects funded by 2025-26 CITRIS Tech for Social Good Program

title: Erica Simpkins is honored for quietly transforming student systems
summary: Erica Simpkins has been selected as this year’s Outstanding Staff Award recipient, recognizing her dedication to transforming how students and staff navigate essential academic and administrative processes.

title: Centering nourishment at The Humanities Institute
summary: The Humanities Institute (THI) at UC Santa Cruz is exploring the theme of nourishment this year, inviting the campus and community to reflect on what sustains life, fosters connection, and makes flourishing possible.“What does it mean to be nourished in our bodies, our minds and our communities at a moment of profound social, political, and ecological change?” said Saskia Nauenberg Dunkell, research programs and communications director for THI.

title: Three UC Santa Cruz professors and one student recognized as Santa Cruz Works ‘Titans’
summary: This recognition celebrates their contributions to the region’s technology, innovation, and entrepreneurship ecosystem.

title: Two UC Santa Cruz alumnae make their Off-Broadway debut
summary: Rosie Glen-Lambert (Porter ’14, theater arts) and Hailey McAfee (Rachel Carson ’13, theater arts) make their Off-Broadway debut at Soho Playhouse from January 14 to February 13. The limited Off-Broadway engagement follows a sold-out run at the Hollywood Fringe Festival where the duo’s production received multiple honors.

summary: UC Santa Cruz experts are working to guide AI in ethical, sustainable, and socially beneficial directions

title: Legendary Jamaican percussionist Larry McDonald to deliver MLK keynote
summary: Artist who recorded with Bob Marley, Lee “Scratch” Perry, Taj Mahal, and Gil Scott-Heron to discuss his seven-decade career in reggae, ska, dub, jazz, soul, and other genres

title: Tiny RNA molecules in sperm, big impact on baby health
summary: Molecular mechanism uncovered in mice by UC Santa Cruz researchers reveals how a father’s diet, stress, and other environmental factors before conception can influence his offspring’s health

title: In Memoriam: Sue Matthews
summary: Sue was a valued member of the UC Santa Cruz community, serving in a variety of roles across the Colleges and Housing from 1999-2023.

title: New book chronicles the life of an enslaved man on the run in the 1700s
summary: David George was born enslaved in Virginia in 1742, but he never gave up on his fight for freedom. Running by night, fording rivers and crossing borders, George embarked on a decades-long odyssey in and out of captivity that carried him thousands of miles. Those repeated getaway form the heart of The Escapes of David George: An Odyssey of Slavery, Freedom, and the American Revolution (St. Martin’s Press), a forthcoming book by History Professor Gregory O’Malley.

title: Javier Gonzalez-Rocha maps air quality with drones
summary: UCSC engineer Javier Gonzalez-Rocha is using drones and sensors to bring advanced air quality monitoring to underserved agricultural communities on California’s Central Coast.

title: From Space to Soil, Alexie Leauthaud Bridges Cosmology and Environmentalism
summary: UC Santa Cruz astronomer Alexie Leauthaud is bringing her cosmology expertise to climate action through Seed Spoon Science, a community-based gardening program focused on environmental justice and sustainability.

title: Launch of Undergraduate Advising Transformation Workgroup
summary: The group will take a close look at how advising is structured and coordinated, identify what’s working well, and explore how new tools, structures, and processes can make the system clearer and more connected for students and advisors alike.

title: Apply for CITRIS STEM weekend workshop: ¡Valle! Get Your Start in Tech!

title: New basic needs directory provides UC students with one stop shop for resource information
summary: With assistance and input from students and UC’s basic needs community of practice, the Center for Economic Justice and Action has launched a directory and an AI-powered chatbot to help UC community members locate critical basic needs resources.

title: Student direct deposit fraud phishing attacks
summary: There is an active phishing attack targeting UC Santa Cruz students. Bad actors are attempting to steal financial aid refunds and disbursements by gaining unauthorized access to student accounts.

title: Bay Tree Building closed for major renovation
summary: The Bay Tree Building is closed as renovations begin to create a welcoming, accessible hub for student connection, services, and community.

title: How quiet galaxies stay quiet: cool gas feeds black holes in ‘red geysers’

title: Finding an affordable path to a UC Santa Cruz degree
summary: Students like Jadin Archambeault and Jacob Chaudhry are actively navigating the cost of college while planning for life after graduation. Their experiences highlight the ways students use financial aid, scholarships, and campus resources to manage costs.

summary: Early on Thursday, Dec. 25, severe winds gusting up to 114 miles per hour tore loose half of the shutter on the 140-year old 36-inch Great Refractor dome. No one was injured.

title: We’re going to need a new innovation ecosystem
summary: The UC system, and other universities across the country are pushing hard to restore federal government funding. Ideally, our advocacy pays off such that sufficient levels of research funding continue to reach universities in support of basic research. Because, without federal support, it’s not just our researchers and students who will suffer—it’s industry, too, and the wider economy and society.

summary: Now in its second year, the QB3 Biotech Undergraduate Summer Internship program at the University of California, Santa Cruz, connects students with hands-on experience in the biotechnology field.
